message:
fix(float): Prevent infinite recursion in toFloat for float subclasses

When a float subclass has __float__ that returns self (e.g., def __float__(self): return self),
the generated Zig code would call runtime.toFloat(__self) which would then call __float__()
again, creating an infinite loop.

Fix: In toFloat(), check for __base_value__ field first before calling __float__() for
float subclasses. This prioritizes the base float value and avoids the recursion.

Also fix support.exceeds_recursion_limit() to return isize (not bool) for compatibility
with CPython tests that use this as a recursion limit value.
